pi.name: David H. Smith IV
pi.bio: David directs the ASCEND Lab, where his research focuses on computing education — particularly how novice developers, data scientists, and designers learn to program. His work spans AI-assisted learning tools, automated assessment, and pedagogical approaches for introductory computing courses.

phd.name: S Moonwara Anjum Monisha
phd.bio: Designing and studying interactive, AI-assisted systems to support students' learning and assessment in introductory computer science, with a focus on database concepts. Working at the intersection of Computing Education Research, Human-Computer Interaction, and AI.

undergrad.name: Ben Mazurek
undergrad.bio: Works on development and testing of code generation based autograding question frameworks.

undergrad.name: Vanshika Punekar
undergrad.bio: Develops PrairieLearn assessment tools using EiPLGrader to improve algorithm comprehension and grading efficiency.

collaborators.name: Paul Denny
collaborators.bio: Co-recipient of the Llama Impact Grant. Research focuses on computing education and AI in education.

collaborators.name: Kaitlin Riegel
collaborators.bio: Co-supervising research on computing education assessment and pedagogical approaches.

collaborators.name: Zihan Wu
collaborators.bio: Research at the intersection of HCI and Computing Education, creating engaging and effective educational tools for computing.

collaborators.name: Minsun Kim
collaborators.bio: Researcher and designer specializing in AI-driven educational technologies. Develops large language model applications that foster personalized learning environments and reimagine the role of teachers in AI-enhanced classrooms.

collaborators.name: Fariha Anjum Shifa
collaborators.bio: Studies how non-native novice programmers can overcome language barriers when learning to code. Using translanguaging theory, her research explores how students draw on all their semiotic and linguistic resources to make sense of programming concepts.
